filters.term: host=172.31.1.9 and apply_overrides=0 and levels=chmlgf and rows=1000 and min_qod=0 and sort-reverse=severity first=1

result.name: Apache HTTP Server End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The Apache HTTP Server version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of the Apache HTTP Server is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the Apache HTTP Server version on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update the Apache HTTP Server version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.description:
The "Apache HTTP Server" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/a:apache:http_server:2.2.34 Installed version: 2.2.34 Location/URL: 80/tcp EOL version: 2.2 EOL date: 2017-12-31

result.name: OpenSSL End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The OpenSSL version on the remote host has reached the end of life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of OpenSSL is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the OpenSSL version on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update the OpenSSL version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.description:
The "OpenSSL" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/a:openssl:openssl:1.0.1t Installed version: 1.0.1t Location/URL: 80/tcp EOL version: 1.0.1 EOL date: 2016-07-06

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.54 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.54 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2022-26377: mod_proxy_ajp: Possible request smuggling
- CVE-2022-28614: Read beyond bounds via ap_rwrite()
- CVE-2022-28615: Read beyond bounds in ap_strcmp_match()
- CVE-2022-29404: Denial of service in mod_lua r:parsebody
- CVE-2022-30556: Information disclosure in mod_lua with websockets
- CVE-2022-31813: mod_proxy X-Forwarded-For dropped by hop-by-hop mechanism|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.53 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.54 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.54 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.54 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.52 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.52 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2022-22719: mod_lua Use of uninitialized value of in r:parsebody
- CVE-2022-22720: HTTP request smuggling vulnerability
- CVE-2022-22721: Possible buffer overflow with very large or unlimited LimitXMLRequestBody
- CVE-2022-23943: mod_sed: Read/write beyond bounds|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.52 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.53 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.53 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.53 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.51 Buffer Overflow Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server <= 2.4.51 Buffer Overflow Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a buffer overflow vulnerability.|insight=A carefully crafted request body can cause a buffer overflow in the mod_lua multipart parser (r:parsebody() called from Lua scripts).|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions through 2.4.51.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.52 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.52 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.52 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.60 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.60 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-36387: Denial of Service (DoS) by Null pointer in websocket over HTTP/2
- CVE-2024-38473: Proxy encoding problem
- CVE-2024-38474: Weakness with encoded question marks in backreferences
- CVE-2024-38475: Weakness in mod_rewrite when first segment of substitution matches filesystem path
- CVE-2024-38476: May use exploitable/malicious backend application output to run local handlers via internal redirect
- CVE-2024-38477: Crash resulting in DoS in mod_proxy via a malicious request
- CVE-2024-39573: mod_rewrite proxy handler substitution|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.59 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.60 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.60 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.60 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL 1.0.2 and 1.0.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities (Sep 2016) - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL 1.0.2 and 1.0.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities (Sep 2016) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=OpenSSL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=OpenSSL suffers from the possibility of multiple vulnerabilities due to:
1) Missing message length checks which results in Out of Bounds reads up to 2 bytes beyond the allocated buffer, this leads to Denial of Service. The attack works only if client authentication is enabled.
2) Calling MDC2_Update() can cause an overflow if an attacker is able to supply very large amounts of input data after a previous call to EVP_EncryptUpdate() with a partial block then a length check can overflow resulting in a heap corruption.
3) A malfored SHA512 TLS session ticket resulting in an Out of Bounds read which leads to service crash.
4) Unchecking the return value of BN_div_word() function causing an Out of Bounds write if it is used with an overly large BIGNUM. TLS is not affected.
5) Misusing OBJ_obj2txt() function by the function TS_OBJ_print_bio() will results in Out of Bounds reads when large OIDs are presented.
6) DTLS out-of-order messages handling which enable an attacker to cause a DoS attack through memory exhaustion.
7) A flaw in the DTLS replay attack protection mechanism enabling the attacker to send records for next epochs with a very large sequence number, this causes in dropping all the subsequent legitimate packets and causing a denial of service for a specific DTLS connection.|affected=OpenSSL 1.0.2 and 1.0.1.|impact=Successful exploitation could result in Denial of Service.|solution=OpenSSL 1.0.2 users should upgrade to 1.0.2i, OpenSSL 1.0.1 users should upgrade to 1.0.1u.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: OpenSSL 1.0.2 users should upgrade to 1.0.2i, OpenSSL 1.0.1 users should upgrade to 1.0.1u.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.0.1u Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.49 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.49 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-34798: NULL pointer dereference in httpd core
- CVE-2021-39275: ap_escape_quotes buffer overflow
- CVE-2021-40438: mod_proxy SSRF|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.48 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.49 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.49 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.49 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.55 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.55 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2006-20001: mod_dav out of bounds read, or write of zero byte
- CVE-2022-36760: Possible request smuggling in mod_proxy_ajp
- CVE-2022-37436: mod_proxy allows a backend to trigger HTTP response splitting|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.54 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.55 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.55 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.55 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Man-in-the-Middle Attack Vulnerability (Jul 2016) - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Man-in-the-Middle Attack Vulnerability (Jul 2016) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a man-in-the-middle attack vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to 'CGI Servlet' does not protect applications from the presence of untrusted client data in the 'HTTP_PROXY' environment variable.|affected=Apache HTTP Server through 2.4.23.
NOTE: Apache HTTP Server 2.2.32 is not vulnerable.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to redirect an application's outbound HTTP traffic to an arbitrary proxy server via a crafted proxy header in an HTTP request.|solution=Update to version 2.4.24, or 2.2.32, or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.24, or 2.2.32, or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.24 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL OCSP Status Request extension unbounded memory growth Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL OCSP Status Request extension unbounded memory growth Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=OpenSSL suffers from the possibility of DoS attack through sending a large OCSP Status Request extensions which lead to unbounded memory growth on the server which in turn lead to denial of service.|affected=OpenSSL 1.1.0 and previous versions.|impact=Successful exploitation could result in service crash.|solution=OpenSSL 1.1.0 users should upgrade to 1.1.0a. OpenSSL 1.0.2 users should upgrade to 1.0.2i. OpenSSL 1.0.1 users should upgrade to 1.0.1u.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: OpenSSL 1.1.0 users should upgrade to 1.1.0a. OpenSSL 1.0.2 users should upgrade to 1.0.2i. OpenSSL 1.0.1 users should upgrade to 1.0.1u.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.0.1u Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL: Integer overflow in CipherUpdate (CVE-2021-23840) - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL: Integer overflow in CipherUpdate (CVE-2021-23840) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=OpenSSL is prone to an integer overflow vulnerability.|insight=Calls to EVP_CipherUpdate, EVP_EncryptUpdate and EVP_DecryptUpdate may overflow the output length argument in some cases where the input length is close to the maximum permissible length for an integer on the platform. In such cases the return value from the function call will be 1 (indicating success), but the output length value will be negative.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.0.2x and prior and 1.1.1i and prior.|impact=This vulnerability could cause applications to behave incorrectly or crash.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2y, 1.1.1j or later. See the references for more details.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 1.0.2y, 1.1.1j or later. See the references for more details.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.0.2y / 1.1.1j Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.58 'mod_macro' Out-of-bounds Read Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.58 'mod_macro' Out-of-bounds Read Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to an out-of-bounds read vulnerability in mod_macro.|insight=|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.57 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.58 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.58 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.58 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Feb 2017)

nvt.name: OpenSSL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Feb 2017)
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=OpenSSL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=Multiple flaws exist due to:
- An out-of-bounds read error while using a specific cipher.
- A carry propagating bug in the x86_64 Montgomery squaring procedure.|affected=OpenSSL 1.1.0 before 1.1.0d and 1.0.2 before 1.0.2k|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a remote attacker to conduct a denial of service attack and produce incorrect results.|solution=Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0d or 1.0.2k or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0d or 1.0.2k or later.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.0.2k Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL Death Alert Denial of Service Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL Death Alert Denial of Service Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to an error in function 'ssl3_read_bytes' in ssl/s3_pkt.c script which might lead to higher CPU usage due to improper handling of warning packets.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.1.0, 1.0.2 through 1.0.2h, all 1.0.1 and all 0.9.8.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a remote attacker to conduct a denial of service condition.|solution=Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.0.2j or 1.1.0b or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.0.2j or 1.1.0b or later.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.1.0b or 1.0.2j Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server OPTIONS Memory Leak Vulnerability (Optionsbleed) - Version Check
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server allows remote attackers to read secret data from process memory if the Limit directive can be set in a user's .htaccess file, or if httpd.conf has certain misconfigurations, aka Optionsbleed.|insight=Optionsbleed is a use after free error in the Apache HTTP Server that causes a corrupted Allow header to be constructed in response to HTTP OPTIONS requests. This can leak pieces of arbitrary memory from the server process that may contain secrets. The memory pieces change after multiple requests, so for a vulnerable host an arbitrary number of memory chunks can be leaked.
The bug appears if a webmaster tries to use the 'Limit' directive with an invalid HTTP method.
Example .htaccess:
<Limit abcxyz> </Limit>|affected=Apache HTTP Server 2.2.x versions up to 2.2.34 and 2.4.x below 2.4.28.|impact=The successful exploitation allows the attacker to read chunks of the host's memory.|solution=Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.28. For Apache HTTP Server running version 2.2.34 apply the patch linked in the references.
As a workaround the usage of .htaccess should be disabled completely via the 'AllowOverride None' directive within the webservers configuration. Furthermore all <Limit> statements within the webserver configuration needs to be verified for invalid HTTP methods.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution:
Update to Apache HTTP Server 2.4.28. For Apache HTTP Server running version 2.2.34 apply the patch linked in the references.
As a workaround the usage of .htaccess should be disabled completely via the 'AllowOverride None' directive within the webservers configuration. Furthermore all <Limit> statements within the webserver configuration needs to be verified for invalid HTTP methods.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: Apply the referenced patch or upgrade to 2.4.28 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.59 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.59 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2023-38709: HTTP response splitting
- CVE-2024-24795: HTTP response splitting in multiple modules
- CVE-2024-27316: HTTP/2 DoS by memory exhaustion on endless continuation frames|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.58 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.59 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.59 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.59 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_proxy_http.c' Denial Of Service Vulnerability

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_proxy_http.c' Denial Of Service Vulnerability
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a Denial of Service vulnerability.|insight=The flaw is due to error in 'stream_reqbody_cl' function in 'mod_proxy_http.c' in the mod_proxy module. When a reverse proxy is configured, it does not properly handle an amount of streamed data that exceeds the Content-Length value via crafted requests.|affected=Apache HTTP Server version prior to 2.3.3.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow remote attackers to cause Denial of Service to the legitimate user by CPU consumption.|solution=Update to version 2.3.3 or later.|vuldetect=|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.3.3 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.3.3 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL: Null pointer deref in X509_issuer_and_serial_hash() (CVE-2021-23841) - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL: Null pointer deref in X509_issuer_and_serial_hash() (CVE-2021-23841) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H|summary=OpenSSL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.|insight=The OpenSSL public API function X509_issuer_and_serial_hash() attempts to create a unique hash value based on the issuer and serial number data contained within an X509 certificate. However it fails to correctly handle any errors that may occur while parsing the issuer field (which might occur if the issuer field is maliciously constructed).|affected=OpenSSL version 1.0.2x and prior and 1.1.1i and prior.|impact=This vulnerability may subsequently result in a NULL pointer deref and a crash leading to a potential denial of service attack.|solution=Update to version 1.0.2y, 1.1.1j or later. See the references for more details.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 1.0.2y, 1.1.1j or later. See the references for more details.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.0.2y / 1.1.1j Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL: Information Disclosure Vulnerability (CVE-2016-7056) - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL: Information Disclosure Vulnerability (CVE-2016-7056) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N|summary=OpenSSL is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists due to an insufficient setting in the 'BN_FLG_CONSTTIME' flag for nonces, which could trigger a failure to take a secure code path in the BN_mod_inverse method that results in a cache-timing condition.|affected=OpenSSL versions 1.0.1u and prior.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a malicious user with local access to recover ECDSA P-256 private keys.|solution=Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0 or 1.0.2 or later. See the references for more details.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0 or 1.0.2 or later. See the references for more details.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.1.0 or 1.0.2 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: OpenSSL 'OOB read' Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: OpenSSL 'OOB read' Security Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.0/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N|summary=OpenSSL is prone to an 'OOB read' security bypass vulnerability.|insight=The flaw exists as OpenSSL could do a one-byte buffer overread if an X.509 certificate has a malformed IPAddressFamily extension.|affected=OpenSSL 1.1.0x prior to 1.1.0g-dev, 1.0.2x prior to 1.0.2m-dev, all 1.0.1x, all 0.9.8x and all 1.0.0x versions.|impact=Successfully exploiting this issue will allow attackers to bypass security restrictions and perform unauthorized actions, this may aid in launching further attacks.|solution=Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0g-dev or 1.0.2m-dev or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Upgrade to OpenSSL version 1.1.0g-dev or 1.0.2m-dev or later.

result.description: Installed version: 1.0.1t Fixed version: 1.1.0g-dev or 1.0.2m-dev Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_lua' Denial of Service Vulnerability -01 (May 2015)

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server 'mod_lua' Denial of Service Vulnerability -01 (May 2015)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:P|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a denial of service vulnerability.|insight=Flaw is due to vulnerability in lua_websocket_read function in lua_request.c in the mod_lua module.|affected=Apache HTTP Server versions through 2.4.12.|impact=Successful exploitation will allow a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via some crafted dimension.|solution=Update to version 2.4.13 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.13 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.13 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.64 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.64 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2024-42516: HTTP response splitting
- CVE-2024-43204: SSRF with mod_headers setting Content-Type header
- CVE-2024-47252: mod_ssl error log variable escaping
- CVE-2025-49812: mod_ssl TLS upgrade attack|affected=Apache HTTP Server version 2.4.63 and prior.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.64 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.64 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.64 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.66 SSI Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server < 2.4.66 SSI Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:M/Au:N/C:N/I:P/A:N|summary=Apache HTTP Server is prone to a Server Side Includes (SSI) vulnerability.|insight=Apache HTTP Server with SSI enabled and mod_cgid (but not mod_cgi) passes the shell-escaped query string to #exec cmd='...' directives.|affected=Apache HTTP Server prior to version 2.4.66.|impact=|solution=Update to version 2.4.66 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update to version 2.4.66 or later.

result.description: Installed version: 2.2.34 Fixed version: 2.4.66 Installation path / port: 80/tcp

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 3280428127 Packet 2: 3280429207

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

result.name: CPE Inventory

nvt.name: CPE Inventory
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: 172.31.1.9|cpe:/a:apache:http_server:2.2.34 172.31.1.9|cpe:/a:apache:mod_ssl:2.2.34 172.31.1.9|cpe:/a:openssl:openssl:1.0.1t 172.31.1.9|cpe:/o:linux:kernel

result.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

nvt.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Best matching OS:
OS: Linux/Unix CPE: cpe:/o:linux:kernel Found by VT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.111067 (Operating System (OS) Detection (HTTP)) Concluded from HTTP Server banner on port 80/tcp: Server: Apache/2.2.34 (Unix) mod_ssl/2.2.34 OpenSSL/1.0.1t DAV/2 Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

result.name: Apache HTTP Server Detection Consolidation

nvt.name: Apache HTTP Server Detection Consolidation
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Consolidation of Apache HTTP Server detections.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Detected Apache HTTP Server
Version: 2.2.34 Location: 80/tcp CPE: cpe:/a:apache:http_server:2.2.34
Concluded from version/product identification result: Server: Apache/2.2.34 (Unix) mod_ssl/2.2.34 OpenSSL/1.0.1t DAV/2

result.name: OpenSSL Detection Consolidation

nvt.name: OpenSSL Detection Consolidation
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Consolidation of OpenSSL detections.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Note: Depending on the install method of OpenSSL on the target host multiple detection and vulnerability results for the same version / installation needs to be expected.|solution_type=

result.description:
Detected OpenSSL
Version: 1.0.1t Location: 80/tcp CPE: cpe:/a:openssl:openssl:1.0.1t
Concluded from version/product identification result: Server: Apache/2.2.34 (Unix) mod_ssl/2.2.34 OpenSSL/1.0.1t DAV/2

result.name: Services

nvt.name: Services
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This plugin performs service detection.|insight=This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: A web server is running on this port

result.name: HTTP Security Headers Detection

nvt.name: HTTP Security Headers Detection
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=All known security headers are being checked on the remote web server.
On completion a report will hand back whether a specific security header has been implemented (including its value and if it is deprecated) or is missing on the target.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: Missing Headers | More Information -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Content-Security-Policy | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#content-security-policy Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy | https://scotthelme.co.uk/coop-and-coep/, Note: This is an upcoming header Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy | https://scotthelme.co.uk/coop-and-coep/, Note: This is an upcoming header Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy | https://scotthelme.co.uk/coop-and-coep/, Note: This is an upcoming header Document-Policy | https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-feature-policy/document-policy#document-policy-http-header Feature-Policy | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#feature-policy, Note: The Feature Policy header has been renamed to Permissions Policy Permissions-Policy | https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-feature-policy/#permissions-policy-http-header-field Referrer-Policy | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#referrer-policy Sec-Fetch-Dest | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 Sec-Fetch-Mode | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 Sec-Fetch-Site | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 Sec-Fetch-User | https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers#fetch_metadata_request_headers, Note: This is a new header supported only in newer browsers like e.g. Firefox 90 X-Content-Type-Options | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-content-type-options X-Frame-Options | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-frame-options X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-permitted-cross-domain-policies X-XSS-Protection | https://owasp.org/www-project-secure-headers/#x-xss-protection, Note: Most major browsers have dropped / deprecated support for this header in 2020.

result.name: HTTP Server Banner Enumeration

nvt.name: HTTP Server Banner Enumeration
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script tries to detect / enumerate different HTTP server banner (e.g. from a frontend, backend or proxy server) by sending various different HTTP requests (valid and invalid ones).|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
It was possible to enumerate the following HTTP server banner(s):
Server banner | Enumeration technique ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Server: Apache/2.2.34 (Unix) mod_ssl/2.2.34 OpenSSL/1.0.1t DAV/2 | Invalid HTTP 00.5 GET request (non-existent HTTP version) to '/'

result.name: HTTP Server type and version

nvt.name: HTTP Server type and version
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script detects and reports the HTTP Server's banner which might provide the type and version of it.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
The remote HTTP Server banner is:
Server: Apache/2.2.34 (Unix) mod_ssl/2.2.34 OpenSSL/1.0.1t DAV/2

result.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting

nvt.name: Web Application Scanning Consolidation / Info Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script consolidates and reports various information for web application (formerly called 'CGI') scanning.
This information is based on the following scripts / settings:
- HTTP-Version Detection (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.100034)
- No 404 check (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10386)
- Web mirroring / webmirror.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.10662)
- Directory Scanner / DDI_Directory_Scanner.nasl (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.11032)
- The configured 'cgi_path' within the 'Scanner Preferences' of the scan config in use
- The configured 'Enable CGI scanning', 'Enable generic web application scanning' and 'Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning' within the 'Global variable settings' of the scan config in use
If you think any of this information is wrong please report it to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
The Hostname/IP "apache22.trabalho_vulnnet" was used to access the remote host.
Generic web application scanning is disabled for this host via the "Enable generic web application scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
Requests to this service are done via HTTP/1.1.
This service seems to be able to host PHP scripts.
This service seems to be able to host ASP scripts.
The User-Agent "Mozilla/5.0 [en] (X11, U; OpenVAS-VT <version replaced>)" was used to access the remote host.
Historic /scripts and /cgi-bin are not added to the directories used for web application scanning. You can enable this again with the "Add historic /scripts and /cgi-bin to directories for CGI scanning" option within the "Global variable settings" of the scan config in use.
The following directories were used for web application scanning:
http://apache22.trabalho_vulnnet/
While this is not, in and of itself, a bug, you should manually inspect these directories to ensure that they are in compliance with company security standards

result.name: Hostname Determination Reporting

nvt.name: Hostname Determination Reporting
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Hostname determination for IP 172.31.1.9:
Hostname|Source apache22.trabalho_vulnnet|Reverse-DNS

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.9):
172.30.0.2 172.31.1.9
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

nvt.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.|solution_type=

result.description: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.
